objective: Implement and verify the OpenAI-compatible chat client + health probe in core::explore::client, including the char-boundary-safe truncate() fix that eliminates the blocking code-review defect.
key_changes: New core/src/explore/client.rs: Role, Message, ToolCall, Tool, ChatRequest, ChatResponse wire model with serde.
ChatClient trait (chat() seam) — T03/T04 depend on this, never the concrete type.
OpenAiCompatClient::new(&ExploreConfig) over existing ureq/rustls — no new dependency.
Dialect normalization: Ollama string-args vs llama.cpp object-args both normalize to the same ToolCall.
ClientError distinguishes Connection (D3 shutdown signal) from Protocol/Http — correct for T03 agent loop.
health_probe GETs {base_url}/models; HealthError::Unreachable vs ModelMissing with tolerant substring/alias matching.
Fixed truncate() — char-boundary-safe walk-back (was &s[..500] which panics on multi-byte UTF-8).
Regression test truncate_does_not_panic_on_multibyte_boundary: 499 ASCII + 50 é straddle the 500-byte cut.
All 5 review-plan advisories addressed: tool_call_id, tolerant model matching, explicit timeouts, error bodies, deterministic 127.0.0.1:1 tests.
12 inline #[cfg(test)] tests; 107 lib tests + 1 doc-test green; cargo clippy -p grove-cst -- -D warnings clean.
core/src/explore/mod.rs: pub mod client; + re-exports of full public surface for T03/T04.
